_readme.purpose: Answer key for the post-wiring RETRIEVAL eval: run each `query` through /rule (or rules.search) and check the returned passage semantically contains `key_facts`. Measures whether the live bot returns CORRECT answers, the true north-star metric that heading/chunking quality only serves.
_readme.how_to_score: For each entry: retrieve top chunk(s) for `query` scoped to `system`; pass if every key_fact is present/paraphrased in a retrieved chunk. Use semantic matching (wording differs by edition), not string equality.

source_tiers.srd-ogl: Open SRD published under OGL — authoritative but Mongoose 1e-derived; verify edition_sensitive entries against the 2E book.
source_tiers.repo-mechanics: Encoded in lorehound/twilight.py (unit-tested); trusted for T2K 4E core mechanics since no free T2K SRD exists.
source_tiers.foundry-vtt: Community Foundry VTT system data (github fria-ligan) — weak authority; only a fallback for T2K gear stats, not yet pulled.

caveats: traveller-srd.com is Mongoose 1e OGL content; your library is Mongoose 2E. Core resolution + characteristic/difficulty DMs are edition-stable; equipment/vehicle/ship-design values are NOT — flagged per entry.
T2K (Free League 4E) has no free authoritative SRD; T2K entries below are core mechanics from twilight.py, not an external source.
_readme.status: wired into scripts/retrieval_eval.py + tests/test_retrieval.py (regression guard). Baseline fact-recall 0.32 (2026-06-22, 8 entries, top-5). 2026-06-26 retrieval overhaul: added 4 T2K targets, then stemming + worked-example rescue + queries calibrated to realistic Discord keyword usage with facts worded to the indexed 2E book (BM25 has no semantic search, per the free/local constraint) → gate ~0.83 over 12 stable entries at top-8 (matching /rule). 2026-07-04 eval-honesty pass: (a) the fuzzy scorer now folds regular -s inflection (adds~add, DMs~DM) so it stops under-counting real matches; (b) the Traveller facts, still carrying MGT1e-SRD wording, were re-aligned to the indexed 2E book (target numbers not DMs; 'unskilled' not 'untrained'), matching the T2K calibration — a diagnostic confirmed the defining passage was already retrieved top-1/2 for every one, so this corrects measurement, not retrieval → gate ~0.95. Residual misses are granular table values / cross-section rules that miss top-k. Expand as new sources arrive; keep queries keyword-style and facts book-aligned.

key_facts: roll 2D
add your skill level
add a characteristic DM
8 or more succeeds

entries.query: What are the task difficulty modifiers?
key_facts: Simple 2+
Easy 4+
Routine 6+
Average 8+
Difficult 10+
Very Difficult 12+
Formidable 14+
entries.notes: Facts worded to the indexed 2E book's TASK DIFFICULTIES table, which states difficulties as TARGET NUMBERS (Average 8+) — MGT1e's SRD stated them as DMs (Average +0). Same difficulty ladder, different representation; the 2E form is what our library contains.

key_facts: not having the skill inflicts an unskilled penalty
the unskilled penalty is DM-3
entries.notes: Worded to the 2E book: it says 'not having the skill... inflicts the usual DM-3 unskilled penalty' (the 1e SRD term was 'untrained').

entries.query: How is the Effect of a roll calculated?
key_facts: subtract 8 from the total (roll + DMs)
margin of success

entries.query: What is the dice modifier for a characteristic value of 9?
key_facts: characteristic 9-11 gives DM +1
entries.notes: Standard MGT characteristic-DM table; confirm exact bands against the 2E core book.

entries.query: How much does Battle Dress cost?
key_facts: ~200,000 Credits (medium chassis, TL 9-10)
+2 Strength
12 equipment slots
entries.notes: Equipment/design value — likely DIFFERS in 2E; verify against the 2E book before trusting.

key_facts: roll 6 or higher on a die to succeed
a roll of 10 or higher counts as two successes
roll the attribute die and the skill die
a 1 is the jam symbol on the die
when you push you re-roll the ammo dice
each 1 when pushing lowers the weapon's reliability
ammo dice are D6 rolled with a ranged attack
you add ammo dice to your firearm skill roll
ammo dice are re-rolled when you push
entries.notes: Facts are the ammo-dice rules stated where 'ammo dice' is defined (add to skill roll; re-roll on push). The prior 'each 6 is an extra hit' fact is the GENERAL success rule (6s = successes = hits), which lives in the dice-rolling section, not the ammo-dice section — so the keyword query 'ammo dice' can't surface it (BM25, no semantic search).

key_facts: roll a D6 for age each term
roll a D8 for age effects
a D8 lower than your number of terms means you decrease an attribute
entries.source: Twilight 2000 4E core, ch.2 (indexed library); encoded in lorehound/chargen/t2k.py
entries.notes: Aging lives only in the chapter's worked example (no Aging rules section). Query + facts use the realistic Discord keyword and the example's own wording: BM25 (no semantic search, per the free/local constraint) needs shared keywords, so a verbose NL paraphrase doesn't retrieve it even though `/rule aging` does.

entries.query: When does World War III break out during character creation?
key_facts: from the second term on, roll a D8 for the war each term
if the roll comes up under your number of terms served, the war breaks out
the war ends your career terms (you take the At War term)
entries.source: Twilight 2000 4E core, ch.2 (indexed library); encoded in lorehound/chargen/t2k.py
entries.notes: Like aging, the war-break-out check appears only in the worked example.

key_facts: gear weight is measured in encumbrance units
one unit is roughly 3kg / an item carried in one hand
an assault rifle is about one unit
smaller items weigh half or a quarter of a unit
entries.notes: Facts read from the indexed core-book prose.

key_facts: you gain rad points when contaminated by radiation
mark rad points on your character sheet
weak radiation is 1 rad per shift
entries.notes: Facts read from the indexed core-book prose.
